full_prompt: Design the chassis and side-panel structure of a model-solar-car benchmark. Regulatory constraints: vehicle must present a cross-section of at least 150 cm^2 to the oncoming airflow on the drive direction; rigid side panels must be at least 100 mm long x 50 mm high; the vehicle interface to the PV array uses a pair of 4 mm banana sockets separated by 18 mm on centerline; vehicle is constrained to a 0.5 mm stainless steel guide wire through a front guide eye and a rear guide slot; total wheelbase between 180 mm and 260 mm. Material: ABS 3D printed or CNC-machined sheet (Young's modulus 2.3 GPa, Poisson 0.35, tensile yield 40 MPa, ultimate 42 MPa, density 1050 kg/m^3). PV array supplied: 6-cell monocrystalline silicon panel of mass 65 g mounted on the top chassis deck spanning at least 180 x 100 mm. Load cases: (LC1) 5x g static factor on total car mass of target 200 g at rest; (LC2) guide-wire lateral pull: 1.5 N at the front guide eye simulating wire drag/deflection; (LC3) PV cantilever: array mass 65 g with 3 g vertical bump applied at the array centroid while the four wheel contact patches are fully fixed; (LC4) 5 minute endurance at 2 Hz cyclic loading at 5% of LC1 representing track vibration for the standard 20 m class A run, 600 cycles. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises below 26 MPa (yield 40 / 1.5) across LC1-LC3, (R2) chassis deflection under array loaded under LC3 below 1.5 mm, (R3) cross-section rule 150 cm^2 and side-panel rules verified geometrically, (R4) total mass below 300 g, (R5) first torsional chassis mode above 40 Hz. Deliverables: STEP AP242, FEA per case, modal, and a geometric-compliance check block.
short_prompt:
Design the chassis and side-panel structure of a model-solar car running on a 0.5 mm stainless guide wire. Material class: ABS (3D-printed or CNC sheet). No external structural standard is cited; design to a 1.5 FoS on yield.

Envelope: frontal cross-section >= 150 cm^2 (rule), rigid side panels >= 100 mm x 50 mm, wheelbase 180-260 mm, PV array 6-cell 65 g supplied, total mass <= 300 g. Target car mass ~200 g.

Load cases.
- LC1 5 g static: 5 g on the 200 g target mass.
- LC2 guide-wire pull: 1.5 N at the front guide eye.
- LC3 PV bump: 3 g vertical at the array centroid with the four wheel contacts fixed.
- LC4 endurance: 600 cycles at 2 Hz of 5% of LC1, representing track vibration over the standard 20 m run.

prompt.title: Model solar car chassis
prompt.brief: ABS chassis for a 200 g model solar car on 0.5 mm guide wire.
